codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly delivers a two-pane studio layout with left source-of-truth panel and right platform workspace. The left panel exposes video preview (with placeholder for missing video), source metadata (title, author, publish date, duration formatted as MM:SS), engagement metrics (likes/comments/shares with locale formatting), transcript reference (with placeholder for missing transcript), and source context (score + recommended flag). The right panel provides a horizontal tab strip for all five V1 platforms with XiaoHongShu active by default, and each tab renders editable title, body/caption (labeled 'Tweet Text' for X), and cover/visual notes fields. Draft state is maintained independently per platform using React component state initialized from loaded item's platformDrafts. The implementation follows project conventions: thin page component, proper use of domain types, no database or prohibited infrastructure. All 20 tests are well-structured and cover the key behaviors including layout rendering, metadata display, tab switching, draft editing, per-platform state isolation, loading existing drafts, and error states. Manual browser verification was thorough, confirming all expected behaviors. Two minor non-blocking observations noted.

issues.description: For non-403 HTTP errors (e.g., 404), the client throws a generic 'Failed to load item' error instead of parsing the API's specific error message. The 403 path correctly reads data.error, but the catch-all !response.ok path discards the API's error detail. This means users see 'Failed to load item' for both 404 and 500 errors. A minor UX gap — consider reading the JSON error body for all error responses.

The draft initialization code accesses data.item.platformDrafts[id] without a null guard on platformDrafts itself. If the API ever returns an item with platformDrafts as null/undefined (e.g., corrupted data), this would throw a runtime error. The type system makes platformDrafts non-optional on ContentItem so this is unlikely in practice, but a defensive check would improve robustness.

Draft state changes in this commit are tracked only in local React component state with no persistence. The commit message and save-indicator placeholder at the bottom of the editor explicitly note this ('Draft changes are tracked locally. Persistence will be implemented in the next feature.'). This is acceptable since the feature scope is the layout/workspace, and persistence is correctly deferred to the next feature (64f178f).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The PLATFORMS constant array is defined inline in the page component file. As more studio features reference these platforms (persistence, checklist, next-step navigation), this constant is duplicated or co-located differently. AGENTS.md doesn't specify where shared UI constants should live, but the architecture doc places shared logic in lib/domain. A convention note about shared UI constants (like platform display metadata) might reduce future co-location decisions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/items/[itemId]/page.tsx:7-13 defines PLATFORMS array inline. The Platform type lives in lib/domain/types.ts but the display labels/descriptions are page-local.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the skill procedure: read mission docs first, invoked test-driven-development, wrote tests before implementation, used agent-browser for manual verification, ran all validators. skillFeedback confirms followedProcedure: true with no deviations. No skill gaps observed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true, deviations is empty. Transcript skeleton shows skill invocations for mission-worker-base and fullstack-web-worker.

summary: The studio-layout-and-platform-workspaces feature is a solid implementation that fully covers VAL-STUDIO-001 through VAL-STUDIO-004. The two-pane layout, source panel, all five V1 platform tabs with XiaoHongShu default, editable draft fields with platform-specific labeling (Tweet Text for X), and independent per-platform state management are all correctly implemented and thoroughly tested with 20 unit tests plus manual browser verification. Three non-blocking issues were noted: generic error messages for non-403 failures, missing defensive null guard on platformDrafts, and no persistence in this commit (correctly deferred to the next feature). The worker followed the skill procedure faithfully. Pass.
